title: Label automations for quick identification
excerpt: When a board has many automations, and they are all the same type, it’s impossible to figure out which automation does what, unless you open each one, a tedious job. Please allow us to name or label the automations so it…

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml:
When a board has many automations, and they are all the same type, it’s impossible to figure out which automation does what, unless you open each one, a tedious job. Please allow us to name or label the automations so it’s easy to identify them.
Thanks!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed.

Feel free to add your vote to this similar previous feature request:

Allow for naming of automation and integration Feature requests
I would like to be able to name or have any other way to preview the “recipe” I created for my own integrations. Its so difficult to manage these because I can’t tell what I created after it is set up.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, yes, yes, please! That is so needed. On some boards it almost impossible to edit automations because they all have the same name and one can’t identify them. A real painpoint.

posts.author: hollie-v (Hollie E VIllaluz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Another addition to this statement would be to move their order or be able to group them.
They currently just come into view as they are created, and my one board has 53 automations and 11 integrations. Going through that can be so daunting that I will procrastinate!

posts.author: richardshusr (Richard Shu)
posts.cookedHtml: I already made this feature request last year. Please bump it up on the priority list.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: I see this is now available in the Board Automations List. However, could I ask that the description field also be in the Automation builder screen? I have to open my automations to see what they are and then go back to the Automations List and scroll back down to the one I just opened.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Very excited by what you wrote @Krishele, but I don’t see where I can add a Description field.

posts.author: jvwls (Julian V.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screen Shot 2020-05-01 at 8.50.23 AM1622×266 30.5 KB

This is where I see it (in the Board Automations list). I noticed it earlier this week, but I don’t recall it being there last week.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: FANTASTIC - thank you @jvwls and monday!!!

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad @jvwls was able to send a screenshot for you. Sorry missed your reply earlier.

posts.author: ben.armytage (Ben Armytage)
posts.cookedHtml: it would be great to be able to intuitively copy automations to similar boards based on the name you gave it too. i.e you can search from your list of named automatons and when you select one it bring that up as the template for you to refine into the new board

posts.author: ben.armytage (Ben Armytage)
posts.cookedHtml: trying to remember which order you put the automations on to work out which one is the right one is not fun

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! We really appreciate you sharing your feedback! Requests from our users are super important to understand the platform needs and continue improving

I’m glad you are finding the automation description field useful!

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Can this be taken a step further? Allow the user to move the Automations up or down, so they can be grouped or put in an order that makes sense, not the order in which they were created.

posts.author: ivanhong-monday.com (ivan-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DF2019,

Ivan here from the monday.com team!

That’s a really great question and while it currently isn’t possible to rearrange the order of the automation recipes that appear in your automation list, I can definitely hear how this could help organize and structure that list to make things more clear.

I’ve gone ahead and passed this feedback to our Autopilot team for them to review and consider this request!

posts.author: MelissaC (Melissa Cheney)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello all- Melissa here from monday! Wanted to say that there are some amazing ideas in this thread relating to how we can improve the experience of using automations within monday. I’ve taken the more recent feedback on this post and relayed it back to our product team internally. As updates come available we will continue to keep you informed.
